description: Get list of all available commands
name: api.getCommands

description: Force an immediate project snapshot to disk and return its absolute path. Call BEFORE any multi-step risky edit so you can roll back atomically with assistant.restore if any subsequent step fails. Optional `label` lets you tag the snapshot for retrieval by name. Snapshots are kept rolling (last 50 per project) under the OS app-data backup directory.
name: assistant.checkpoint

label.description: Optional human-readable tag for the checkpoint (alphanumerics + dash/underscore, up to 32 chars after sanitisation).

description: Resolve a dataset by path, title, or uniqueId in one call -- dispatches to project.dataset.getByPath / .getByTitle / .getByUniqueId depending on which key is set. Exactly one of {path, title, uniqueId} must be provided.
name: assistant.dataset.resolve

title.description: Dataset title (may be ambiguous if duplicate titles exist).

uniqueId.description: Opaque uniqueId from a prior snapshot/list call.

description: List the rolling backup snapshots for the currently loaded project, newest first. Returns `{checkpoints: [{path, timestamp, sizeBytes, label}], count, directory}`. The `directory` field is the absolute backup folder path -- useful for surfacing in UI escape hatches.
name: assistant.listCheckpoints

limit.description: Cap the number of entries returned (default 20, max 50).

description: Run several project mutations atomically with respect to autosave. Identical contract to project.batch (max 1024 ops, stopOnError default false, NOT a database transaction) but pre-validates that no op tries to nest another batch. Pass dryRun:true at the top level to preview every op without committing -- forwarded to project.batch which rejects the dryRun when any op is not in the dryRun-aware command set.
name: assistant.project.bulkApply

ops.description: Array of {command, params} ops. Max 1024. Nested project.batch / assistant.project.bulkApply rejected.

description: Restore a previously taken checkpoint, replacing the current project state. Provide one of: `path` (absolute path returned by assistant.checkpoint or from a delete response's `backupPath`), `timestamp` (ISO string from assistant.listCheckpoints), or `label` (most recent checkpoint matching this label). DESTRUCTIVE: any unsaved current state is overwritten -- a defensive pre-restore snapshot is taken and returned as `reverseSnapshotPath` so you can undo the restore itself.
name: assistant.restore

path.description: Absolute path to the snapshot file.

description: Dry-run a script then write it via the matching setCode endpoint. Falls back to dryCompile for frame_parser when no inputBytes(Hex) is supplied, and defaults transform values to [0.0] so callers can apply without test inputs. Returns steps[] so the LLM can see exactly which stage ran. If the dry run fails, setCode is NOT called.
name: assistant.script.apply

description: Validate a script by kind without writing. Routes to project.frameParser.dryRun, project.dataset.transform.dryRun, project.painter.dryRun, project.outputWidget.dryRun, or project.dryRun.endToEnd based on `kind`. All other params are forwarded verbatim (code, language, values, inputValue / hex for output_widget, sampleFrame / sampleFrames for end_to_end, plus for frame_parser the pipeline inputs inputBytesHex / decoderMethod / frameDetection / frameStart / frameEnd / hexadecimalDelimiters / checksumAlgorithm / operationMode).
name: assistant.script.dryRun

code.description: Script source. Required for every kind except end_to_end, which defaults to the live project parser.

kind.description: One of: frame_parser, transform, painter, output_widget, end_to_end.

description: Token-efficient project snapshot for LLMs. Identical payload to project.snapshot -- prefer this name in skill docs so the assistant rails stay grouped. Optional verbose=true includes parser code and per-source frame settings.
name: assistant.snapshot

verbose.description: When true, include parser code and per-source frame settings.

description: Add a tile to a workspace in one call. Chains: resolve dataset (by path or uniqueId) -> resolve or create workspace -> setCustomizeMode(true) -> setOptions to enable the matching DatasetOption bit -> patch ranges (pltMin/pltMax/wgtMin/wgtMax/fftMin/fftMax/ledHigh) -> addWidget. Returns steps[] so the LLM can see which sub-call failed. For group-level widgets (datagrid/multiplot/led/accelerometer/gyroscope/gps/plot3d/painter/imageview/output-panel), omit dataset/uniqueId and pass groupId directly.
name: assistant.workspace.addTile

createWorkspace.description: When true and the workspace title doesn't exist, create it.

groupId.description: Group id (required for group-level widgets when no dataset is given).

ranges.description: Optional {pltMin, pltMax, wgtMin, wgtMax, fftMin, fftMax, ledHigh} -- only keys present are written.

uniqueId.description: Dataset uniqueId (per-dataset widgets, alternative to dataset).

widgetType.description: Widget slug (plot, fft, gauge, ...) or DashboardWidget integer.

description: Plan a workspace edit: returns the (groupId, widgetType) tuples whose compatibleWidgetTypeSlugs match the optional widgetType filter. Use this before assistant.workspace.addTile to discover which groups can host a given visualization. Falls back to listing every compatible tuple when no widgetType filter is set.
name: assistant.workspace.plan

widgetType.description: Optional: only return tuples compatible with this slug (plot, fft, gauge, ...) or DashboardWidget integer.

description: Resolve a workspace by id or case-insensitive title. Returns the matching workspace summary or an error with candidates[] for ambiguous / missing lookups.
name: assistant.workspace.resolve

description: Validate control-script source WITHOUT installing or running it (params: code). Compiles the script in a sandboxed engine with the SDK prelude, reports syntax errors with line numbers, and checks that setup() and/or loop() are defined. setup()/loop() are never executed and apiCall/tableSet have no effect. Use before controlScript.set.
name: controlScript.dryRun

description: Get the project's setup()/loop() control script source code.

description: Get the control script source (alias of controlScript.get; matches the project.frameParser.getCode naming convention).
name: controlScript.getCode

description: Returns whether the control script is currently running.
name: controlScript.getStatus

description: Replace the project's control script source (params: code). The script is persisted in the project and applied to the live runtime; if a device is connected it is recompiled and restarted immediately. Validate first with controlScript.dryRun.
name: controlScript.set

description: Replace the control script source (alias of controlScript.set; params: code; matches the project.frameParser.setCode naming convention).
name: controlScript.setCode

description: Re-run every dataset transform from the last received values and republish the frames to the dashboard (no export side effects). Dataset transforms normally run only when a device frame arrives; call this after project.dataTable.setValue writes (control scripts: tableSet()) so the dashboard reflects them while the device is silent.
name: dashboard.reprocess

description: Set the visualization refresh rate (params: fps - 1-240 Hz)
name: dashboard.setFps

description: Set the operation mode (params: mode - 0=ProjectFile, 1=ConsoleOnly, 2=QuickPlot)
name: dashboard.setOperationMode

description: Set the visible plot time window (params: seconds - 0.001-300)
name: dashboard.setTimeRange

seconds.description: Visible plot time window in seconds (0.001-300)

description: Return the last N samples (timestamp + value pairs) for each plot-enabled dataset. Use to debug "the values look wrong" complaints without polling dashboard.getData repeatedly. Only returns datasets whose plt flag is on; toggle it via project.dataset.setOption first if you need history for a different dataset.
name: dashboard.tailFrames

uniqueIds.description: Optional filter: only datasets whose uniqueId is in this list. Empty/missing = every plot-enabled dataset.

description: Force a dashboard render from the current table/dataset state, synthesizing the project frame structure if no device frame has arrived yet. Unlike dashboard.reprocess (which no-ops until a real frame exists and never feeds exporters), this lets a control script that only writes tables (tableSet()) render table-driven virtual datasets from the very first loop(), and it also fans the synthesized frame out to the enabled export sinks (CSV/MDF4/session/MQTT/API). SDK: dashboardTick().
name: dashboard.tick

description: Open the I/O connection using the active bus type's currently-configured device + parameters. Configure FIRST -- the active bus's setters (io.uart.setDevice + setBaudRate, io.network.setRemoteAddress + setTcpPort, io.ble.selectDevice, etc.) must already be set. Use io.getStatus to verify configurationOk before connecting. Idempotent if already connected to the same device.
name: io.connect

description: Close the active I/O connection. Safe to call when already disconnected. Does NOT reset the configuration -- the next io.connect will reuse the same device + params.
name: io.disconnect

description: Returns the latest RAW frame received from the device: sequence number, payload (text/base64) and the parser's channel tokens in values[] -- including channels NOT yet mapped to any dataset (dashboard.getData only shows mapped data). Poll it from a control script loop() to detect new channels and auto-build widgets via project mutations; the sequence field tells you when a new frame arrived. Capture runs only while a control script is running or the API server is enabled, so hasData:false means no data OR no active consumer. timestampMs is a monotonic clock in milliseconds (not Unix epoch time); use it only for deltas between frames. ageMs is the time elapsed since the frame was captured -- use it for staleness watchdogs instead of mixing clocks.
name: io.getLatestFrame

encoding.description: Payload encoding in the response (default: text).

sourceId.description: Optional source filter; omit for the newest frame across all sources.

description: Returns the current connection state: isConnected, paused, busType + busTypeLabel, configurationOk (true when the active bus has enough config to call io.connect), readOnly/readWrite capability flags. Call this first when the user reports a connection issue.
name: io.getStatus

description: Create a new outgoing-action button shown on the toolbar. Actions transmit a configurable payload (text or binary, with optional EOL sequence) to the device on click, or repeat on a timer. After creation, populate it with project.action.update {actionId, title, txData, eolSequence, timerMode (0=Off, 1=AutoStart, 2=ToggleOnTrigger), timerIntervalMs, repeatCount, icon}. Common uses: 'send AT command', 'request telemetry', 'reset device'.

Unknown fields are accepted but ignored, and surfaced in result.warnings[].fields with code 'unknown_field'.
name: project.action.update

description: Load current project into FrameBuilder
name: project.activate

description:
Run several project mutations atomically WITH RESPECT TO AUTOSAVE -- all ops execute sequentially under one suspended-autosave window, and a single save is flushed at the end. Use this whenever you would otherwise issue more than ~5 sequential mutations (renames, retypes, reindexes), since N round-trips cost N times the latency and N times the autosave/tree-rebuild churn.
Each op is {command: '<registered command name>', params: {...}}; results are returned in the same order with per-op success/error fields. Set stopOnError:true to abort the batch on the first failure (default false: best-effort, all ops attempted).
Pass dryRun:true at the top level to preview every op without committing. Each op's per-result still carries the affected-entity payload as if it had run. Rejected when any op is not in the dryRun-aware command set -- mixing previewable and non-previewable ops would leave a partial mutation, which is worse than no preview at all.
Note: NOT transactional. Already-applied ops are NOT rolled back on later failures -- this is a save-suspend wrapper, not a database transaction. Nested project.batch calls are rejected. Hard cap of 1024 ops per call.
Example: rename 40 datasets in one round-trip:
  ops: [
    {command:'project.dataset.update', params:{groupId:0, datasetId:0, title:'LED 1', index:1}},
    {command:'project.dataset.update', params:{groupId:0, datasetId:1, title:'LED 2', index:2}},
    ...
  ]
name: project.batch

ops.description: Array of {command, params} ops to execute sequentially. Max 1024.

command.description: Registered command name (e.g. 'project.dataset.update'). Not 'project.batch' -- nested batches are rejected.

params.description: Arguments object for the command, exactly what you would pass at the top level if calling it directly.

description: Get the visible plot time window (alias of dashboard.getTimeRange).
name: project.dashboard.getTimeRange

description: Set the visible plot time window (alias of dashboard.setTimeRange; the value is per-project and survives project reload).
name: project.dashboard.setTimeRange

seconds.description: Visible plot time window in seconds (0.001-300)

description: Create a new empty table (params: name="Shared Table")
name: project.dataTable.add

description: Append a register (params: table, name, computed=true, defaultValue=0)
name: project.dataTable.addRegister

computed.description: true=computed (writable by transforms, persists across frames), false=constant

description: Delete a table and all its registers (params: name). Pass dryRun:true to return the register list that WOULD be deleted without committing.
name: project.dataTable.delete

dryRun.description: If true, return what would be deleted without committing.

description: Delete a register (params: table, name). Pass dryRun:true to return what WOULD be deleted without committing.
name: project.dataTable.deleteRegister

dryRun.description: If true, return what would be deleted without committing.

description: Return the register list for a table (params: name)
name: project.dataTable.get

description: Return a register's LIVE runtime value from the data-table store (params: table, name). This is the same store the parser/transform tableGet() reads; control scripts use it through the tableGet() global.
name: project.dataTable.getValue

description: List all user-defined data tables
name: project.dataTable.list

description: Rename a table (params: oldName, newName)
name: project.dataTable.rename

description: Write a register's LIVE runtime value into the data-table store (params: table, name, value). Same effect as a parser/transform tableSet(); control scripts use it through the tableSet() global. Constant registers reject writes.
name: project.dataTable.setValue

description: Update a register (params: table, name, newName?, computed?, defaultValue?)
name: project.dataTable.updateRegister

description:
Add a dataset to a group. A dataset is one channel of incoming data: it gets one column in CSV/MDF4 export, one entry in the frame parser's output array (its `index` field), and zero or more dashboard widgets driven by the `options` bitflags.
Pass `options` as the bitwise OR of the visualizations you want:
  1 = Plot (time-series line)
  2 = FFT (frequency-domain plot)
  4 = Bar
  8 = Gauge (radial dial; needs widgetMin/widgetMax)
  16 = Compass (heading 0-360)
  32 = LED (binary indicator with ledHigh threshold)
  64 = Waterfall (Pro; spectrogram, FFT-driven)
  0 = no widget (raw column for export only)
Combine: 1|8 = 9 = plot AND gauge. After creation, set title, units, ranges, and transformCode via project.dataset.update.
name: project.dataset.add

groupId.description: Group to attach the dataset to

options.description: Visualization bit flags. See description for decision guidance.

description:
Bulk-create N datasets in one call -- the right tool whenever you would otherwise loop project.dataset.add. Avoids per-call overhead and the autosave-debounce churn that comes with rapid mutation bursts. After creation, individual datasets can still be patched with project.dataset.update or another project.batch round-trip.
  count          -- how many datasets to create (1..1024).
  options        -- visualization bitfield (same as project.dataset.add).
  titlePattern   -- optional, e.g. 'LED {n}'. {n} is replaced with the running counter (startNumber + i), {i} with the zero-based index. Omit to keep the auto-generated title from project.dataset.add.
  startNumber    -- optional, default 1; first {n} value.
  startIndex     -- optional, default -1 (auto-assign next free parser slot). Pass 0 to leave index unset, or 1+ to assign consecutive parser slots starting from there.
Returns {count, created: [{datasetId, title, index, uniqueId}...]}.
name: project.dataset.addMany

groupId.description: Group to attach the datasets to

options.description: Visualization bit flags. See project.dataset.add for the bit table.

description: Delete a dataset by id. Pass dryRun:true to preview the renumbering without committing -- the response carries the same {deleted, renumbered, warnings} shape as a real call, plus a top-level dryRun:true flag. Always preview before committing when destructive intent is even slightly uncertain.
name: project.dataset.delete

dryRun.description: If true, return the affected entities without committing. Auto-runs without an approval card.

description: Duplicate a dataset by id (params: groupId, datasetId)
name: project.dataset.duplicate

description: Returns the dataset's coloured alarm bands as a JSON array. Each entry: {min, max, severity (0=Info, 1=OK, 2=Warning, 3=Critical), color ("#rrggbb" override or empty for severity default), label, blink}. Also returns rangeMin/rangeMax (the dataset's wgtMin/wgtMax) so callers can validate band ranges before writing back. An empty array means no alarms configured. Applies to bar / gauge / meter widgets and LED-panel datasets; calling on other widget types succeeds with an empty array.
name: project.dataset.getAlarmBands

description: Resolve a dataset by title path. Preferred for human-readable addressing -- survives uniqueId reordering.
name: project.dataset.getByPath

description: Resolve a dataset by exact title. Pass sourceId / groupId to disambiguate when titles repeat across groups.
name: project.dataset.getByTitle

description: Resolve a dataset by its uniqueId. Returns the same shape as the elements of project.dataset.list.
name: project.dataset.getByUniqueId

uniqueId.description: Opaque persisted handle allocated at dataset creation; stable across reorders. Read it from list/snapshot responses, never compute it.

description: Returns the order datasets execute in during transform processing. Useful for debugging cross-dataset transforms (a transform reads final values only for datasets earlier in this list).
name: project.dataset.getExecutionOrder

description: List all datasets across all groups
name: project.dataset.list

description: Reorder a dataset within its group. Changes datasetId (and therefore uniqueId) for the moved dataset and any it crossed; workspace refs re-anchor automatically. Scripts that pinned a uniqueId must be updated -- prefer dataset.getByPath in scripts. Pass dryRun:true to preview the renumbering without committing.
name: project.dataset.move

dryRun.description: If true, return the affected entities without committing. Auto-runs without an approval card.

newPosition.description: New 0-based position within the group; clamped to valid range.

description:
Atomically replaces the dataset's alarmBands array. Each band entry must provide numeric min and max (max>min; bands with max<=min are silently dropped and counted in result.droppedInvalid), and an integer severity (0=Info, 1=OK, 2=Warning, 3=Critical). color is optional ("#rrggbb" override; empty/missing = severity's theme colour). label is optional (surfaces in band-edge notifications). blink is optional (boolean; LED panels flash the LED while the band is active). Bands may have gaps and may overlap; rendering paints them in array order behind the value indicator. Severity >= Warning triggers a notification when the value enters the band, even when no widget is visible (3-second per-dataset cooldown suppresses oscillation spam).
Pass an empty array to clear all alarms.
name: project.dataset.setAlarmBands

alarmBands.description: Full replacement band list (empty = no alarms).

blink.description: Optional; LED panels flash the LED while this band is active

color.description: Optional "#rrggbb" override; empty = use severity's theme colour

max.description: Upper bound (exclusive at top of range)

description: DEPRECATED -- prefer project.dataset.setOptions, which takes the full bitfield in one call and removes the singular/plural ambiguity. Kept for backward compatibility with existing scripts. Toggles one DatasetOption flag on a dataset and updates the group's compatibleWidgetTypes immediately. option values (DatasetOption bitflag): 1=Plot, 2=FFT, 4=Bar, 8=Gauge, 16=Compass, 32=LED, 64=Waterfall (Pro). Bar/Gauge/Compass are mutually exclusive. NOTE: this is a DatasetOption bitflag, NOT a DashboardWidget enum -- the numbers do not line up with project.workspace.addWidget's widgetType.
name: project.dataset.setOption

enabled.description: Whether to enable or disable the option

option.description: PREFERRED: a slug -- 'plot', 'fft', 'bar', 'gauge', 'compass', 'led', 'waterfall'. Integer DatasetOption bitflag still accepted (1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64).

description: Apply several DatasetOption flags at once (plural form of project.dataset.setOption). Pass `options` as the bitwise OR of the flags you want enabled; any flag NOT set in the value is disabled. Bits: 1=Plot, 2=FFT, 4=Bar, 8=Gauge, 16=Compass, 32=LED, 64=Waterfall (Pro). Bar/Gauge/Compass are mutually exclusive -- if more than one is set, the highest bit wins. Updates the group's compatibleWidgetTypes immediately. NOTE: these are DatasetOption bitflags, NOT DashboardWidget enum values -- the numbers do not line up with project.workspace.addWidget's widgetType.
name: project.dataset.setOptions

options.description: PREFERRED: an array of slugs (e.g. ['plot','fft','waterfall']). Integer bitflag still accepted (Plot=1, FFT=2, Bar=4, Gauge=8, Compass=16, LED=32, Waterfall=64).

description: Set dataset transformCode. Empty clears. Pass `language` whenever you author code so the dataset's transformLanguage matches the syntax you wrote -- mismatches compile-fail silently. Lua (1) is the recommended default; it's measurably faster than JavaScript on hot transforms. If this dataset is compute-only (no slot in the parser output array), also set virtual=true via project.dataset.setVirtual or project.dataset.update. Validate with project.dataset.transform.dryRun before setting. **Call meta.fetchScriptingDocs{kind: 'transform_lua' | 'transform_js'} first** for the transform(value) signature, table API (tableGet/tableSet/datasetGetRaw/datasetGetFinal), and execution-order rules -- a transform may read RAW values from any dataset but only FINAL values of datasets earlier in project.dataset.getExecutionOrder.
name: project.dataset.setTransformCode

code.description: Transform source (Lua or JS, must match `language`)

language.description: Optional: 0=JavaScript, 1=Lua (recommended). If omitted, the dataset inherits the source's frameParserLanguage.

description: Toggle the virtual flag on a dataset (params: groupId, datasetId, virtual)
name: project.dataset.setVirtual

virtual.description: Mark dataset as virtual (computed by transform)

description: Compile and execute a value-transform script against one or more sample inputs WITHOUT touching the live project. Returns the per-input transform output or compile errors. Params: code (string), language (0=JS, 1=Lua), values (array of numbers or strings).
name: project.dataset.transform.dryRun

values.description: Sample values to pass through transform(). Each entry may be a number or a string -- the dispatcher coerces as needed.

description:
Patch dataset fields by id (params: groupId, datasetId, plus any of title, units, widget, index, sourceId, graph, fft, led, waterfall, log, overviewDisplay, hideOnDashboard, xAxisId, waterfallYAxis, fftSamples, fftSamplingRate, fftMin, fftMax, pltMin, pltMax, wgtMin, wgtMax, ledHigh, alarmBands (array of {min,max,severity,color,label,blink} objects, severity=0..3 for Info/Ok/Warning/Critical), or legacy alarmLow/alarmHigh/alarmEnabled for 2-band simple mode, displayTickCount, displayFormat, decimalPoints, transformCode, transformLanguage, virtual). The boolean fields graph/fft/led/waterfall toggle the same flags as project.dataset.setOption -- use them here when patching multiple fields at once.
Unknown fields are accepted but ignored, and surfaced in result.warnings[].fields with code 'unknown_field' so the caller can self-correct on the next turn instead of silently mis-applying a typo.
REMINDERS for compute-only datasets:
  - Set virtual=true when the dataset's value comes from transformCode rather than from a slot in the parser output. Without virtual=true the dataset still tries to read channels[index-1] and ends up empty.
  - `index` is the 1-based parser-output slot (0 = unassigned). Patchable via this call; bulk-renumbering 40 datasets is best done through project.batch to avoid round-trip overhead.
  - Set transformLanguage explicitly (0=JavaScript, 1=Lua, -1=inherit from source). Mismatched language vs code = silent compile failure. Lua is the recommended default; it's faster on the hotpath.
name: project.dataset.update

description: End-to-end dry run: takes a sample frame body, runs the project's frame parser, then applies every dataset's transform in execution order, and returns the final per-dataset values WITHOUT touching live state. Required: sampleFrame (single body) OR sampleFrames (array of bodies); everything else defaults to the live project. Use this to verify the full parse->transform pipeline before issuing setCode/setTransformCode. Note: the table API (tableGet/tableSet/datasetGetRaw/datasetGetFinal) is NOT injected; transforms that depend on it should be tested with project.dataset.transform.dryRun individually.
name: project.dryRun.endToEnd

code.description: Optional override for the frame parser source (default: use live project)

language.description: Optional override: 0 = JavaScript, 1 = Lua (default: live source language)

sampleFrame.description: Single frame body (without delimiters). Use sampleFrames for an array. One of sampleFrame / sampleFrames is required.

sampleFrames.description: Array of frame bodies; runs sequentially in one parser engine instance. One of sampleFrame / sampleFrames is required.

sourceId.description: Source index to use for parser code + dataset transforms (default 0)

verbose.description: Include raw cell values alongside final transformed values (default false)

description: Export project as JSON
name: project.exportJson

description: Compile-only check for a frame parser. Catches syntax errors and the 'wrong-language' silent failure (e.g. Lua code passed with language=0). Returns {ok, error?, warning?} without executing the parser. Cheap; use before frameParser.setCode when authoring.
name: project.frameParser.dryCompile

description: Compile and execute frame parser code against raw stream bytes WITHOUT touching the live project. Drives the full pipeline: extraction (delimiters / detection) -> decoder switch -> parse(). Identical code path to the live FrameBuilder and the frame parser test dialog. Required: code, language, inputBytesHex (preferred; binary-safe) or inputBytes (UTF-8 text). Recommended: decoderMethod + frameDetection + frameStart / frameEnd + checksumAlgorithm, otherwise sensible defaults apply (PlainText, EndDelimiterOnly, no delimiters, no checksum). Returns per-frame raw / decoder / rows plus extractedCount / consumedBytes / remainingBytes / droppedFrames. For binary protocols (COBS, Modbus, custom binary) pick decoderMethod=3 (Binary); the text decoders run through QString::fromUtf8 and corrupt non-ASCII bytes.
name: project.frameParser.dryRun

checksumAlgorithm.description: Checksum name to validate trailing bytes. Empty (default) = none.

decoderMethod.description: 0=PlainText (default; UTF-8 -> QString, mojibakes binary), 1=Hexadecimal (toHex -> QString), 2=Base64 (toBase64 -> QString), 3=Binary (raw QByteArray, only mode that's safe for binary protocols).

frameEnd.description: End delimiter. Hex when hexadecimalDelimiters is true. Default: none.

frameStart.description: Start delimiter. Hex when hexadecimalDelimiters is true. Default: none.

hexadecimalDelimiters.description: When true, frameStart / frameEnd are parsed as hex bytes. Default false.

inputBytes.description: Raw stream bytes as UTF-8 text. Lossy for binary payloads -- prefer inputBytesHex for COBS / Modbus / non-ASCII. One of inputBytes / inputBytesHex must be a non-empty string.

inputBytesHex.description: Raw stream bytes as a hex string (space-tolerant). Binary-safe; use this for COBS or any non-ASCII protocol. One of inputBytes / inputBytesHex must be a non-empty string.

operationMode.description: 0=ProjectFile (default; runs decoder + parser), 2=QuickPlot (line extractor, comma-split, parser is bypassed). 1=ConsoleOnly is invalid for dryRun.

description: Read the current frame parser source for a given data source. Returns {code, language}; Built-In sources also return {template, params} and `code` carries the JSON descriptor. Always read BEFORE rewriting -- preserve the user's existing structure where reasonable.
name: project.frameParser.getCode

description: Get frame parser configuration
name: project.frameParser.getConfig

description: Get the script language used by the frame parser for a given source
name: project.frameParser.getLanguage

description: Get the Built-In frame parser configuration for a source. Returns {sourceId, language, template, params}; template is empty when the source never used the Built-In language.
name: project.frameParser.getTemplate

description: Get the parameter schema of a Built-In frame parser template. Returns {id, name, description, params: [{key, type, label, description, default, options?, min?, max?}]}. Param types: string, char, int, float, bool, enum (enum values come from options[].value).
name: project.frameParser.getTemplateSchema

description: List the Built-In (C++) frame parser templates. Returns {templates: [{id, name, description}], count}. Built-In templates parse without user code and are the fastest option; configure one with project.frameParser.setTemplate after inspecting its parameters via project.frameParser.getTemplateSchema.
name: project.frameParser.listTemplates

description: Set frame parser code (params: code, optional sourceId, optional language). Always pass `language` when authoring code to lock in the runtime engine -- mismatch = silent compile failure. Lua (1) is the recommended default; it's faster than JavaScript on the hotpath at typical telemetry rates. Use JavaScript only when you need a JS-specific library or feature. Validate with project.frameParser.dryRun (or dryCompile for a syntax-only check) before setCode. **Call meta.fetchScriptingDocs{kind: 'frame_parser_lua' | 'frame_parser_js'} first** for the parse() signature, return-shape rules, and the tableGet/tableSet API. For Built-In (2), prefer project.frameParser.setTemplate; passing the JSON descriptor as `code` also works.
name: project.frameParser.setCode

code.description: Frame parser script code (JS or Lua), or for the Built-In language the JSON descriptor {"template": id, "params": {...}}

language.description: Optional: 0 = JavaScript, 1 = Lua, 2 = Built-In (parametrized C++ template). When supplied, the source language is flipped before the code is validated and script errors are returned as API errors.

description: Switch a source between JavaScript, Lua and Built-In frame parsers. WARNING: for JS/Lua this WIPES any existing frameParser code for that source -- the loaded default template for the new language replaces it. If you want to preserve+translate, frameParser.getCode first, switch, then frameParser.setCode with the translated source. Switching to Built-In (2) seeds the default 'delimited' template and leaves JS/Lua code intact for round-trips.
name: project.frameParser.setLanguage

language.description: Script language: 0 = JavaScript, 1 = Lua, 2 = Built-In (parametrized C++ template)

description: Select a Built-In frame parser template for a source and switch the source to the Built-In language. Params are validated against the template schema; omitted params use the schema defaults. Use project.frameParser.dryRun (language 2, descriptor as code) to preview the output before or after applying.
name: project.frameParser.setTemplate

params.description: Template parameters (see getTemplateSchema); omitted keys use schema defaults

description: Configure frame parser settings (params: startSequence, endSequence, checksumAlgorithm, frameDetection, operationMode)
name: project.frameParser.update

description: Returns top-level project state: title, file path, modified flag, operation mode (ProjectFile/ConsoleOnly/QuickPlot), counts of groups/datasets/sources/actions/workspaces. Useful as a sanity check before destructive operations.
name: project.getStatus

description:
Create a new visualization group. Pick widgetType by data shape:
  - 5 (NoGroupWidget): just hold related datasets together; per-dataset widgets render individually. Default for arbitrary scalar data.
  - 4 (MultiPlot): N values plotted on a shared time axis. The right choice for correlated signals (sensor-array, multi-channel ADC).
  - 0 (DataGrid): tabular numeric readout. Good for long lists of scalars where graphing isn't useful.
  - 1/2/3 (Accelerometer / Gyroscope / GPS): typed 3-axis IMU or GPS group. Datasets must follow conventional widget tags ("x", "y", "z" for IMUs; "lat", "lon", "alt" for GPS).
  - 6 (Plot3D, Pro): 3D point trail from three datasets.
  - 7 (ImageView, Pro): displays an embedded JPEG/PNG stream.
  - 8 (Painter, Pro): user-scripted JS canvas. Group can be EMPTY (no datasets) and read peer datasets via datasetGetFinal(uniqueId). See meta.howTo('add_painter').
Don't pick 0 / DataGrid as a default -- it makes a forgettable table. Match the user's data.
name: project.group.add

title.description: Group title shown in dashboard headers and the Project Editor tree

widgetType.description: GroupWidget enum -- see command description for decision guidance. 0=DataGrid, 1=Accelerometer, 2=Gyroscope, 3=GPS, 4=MultiPlot, 5=NoGroupWidget, 6=Plot3D, 7=ImageView, 8=Painter

description: Delete a group by id. Pass dryRun:true to preview what would change without committing -- the response contains the same {deleted, renumbered, warnings} fields as a real call, plus a top-level dryRun:true flag. Always preview before committing when the user doesn't have a backup workflow.
name: project.group.delete

dryRun.description: If true, return the affected entities without committing. Auto-runs without an approval card.

description: Duplicate a group by id (params: groupId)
name: project.group.duplicate

description: List all groups with dataset counts
name: project.group.list

description: Reorder a group within the project. Changes groupId for the moved group and any it crossed (which propagates to dataset uniqueIds). Workspace refs re-anchor automatically; scripts pinning a uniqueId must be updated. Pass dryRun:true to preview the renumbering.
name: project.group.move

dryRun.description: If true, return the affected entities without committing. Auto-runs without an approval card.

newPosition.description: New 0-based position; clamped to valid range.

description: Patch group fields by id (params: groupId, plus any of title, widget, columns, sourceId, painterCode). Unknown fields are accepted but ignored, and surfaced in result.warnings[].fields with code 'unknown_field'.
name: project.group.update

description: Replace the current project with a JSON object IN MEMORY (no file association). Use when you have a project shape ready to install -- e.g. building a project from scratch in one shot, or implementing a custom template. The JSON shape must match the .ssproj schema (top-level: title, frameStart, frameEnd, frameDetection, decoder, frameParser, groups, actions, ...). Prefer project.template.apply for canned starters. Pass dryRun:true to return wouldDiscard + wouldApply summaries without loading.
name: project.loadJson

dryRun.description: If true, summarize the config without loading it.

description: Read the MQTT publisher configuration (broker, mode, TLS, etc.). The password is NEVER returned -- inspect 'hasCredentials' instead. Use 'modes', 'mqttVersions', 'sslProtocols', 'peerVerifyModes' as the canonical enum tables when interpreting the integer fields.
name: project.mqtt.publisher.getConfig

description: Snapshot publisher live state (connected, messagesSent, broker endpoint). Does NOT trigger a new connection attempt.
name: project.mqtt.publisher.getStatus

description: Patch one or more MQTT publisher fields. Pass only the keys you want to change. Setting 'password' requires 'username' in the same call -- the credential pair is persisted to the encrypted vault, NOT to the project file. To clear credentials, pass empty strings for both. Setting 'enabled:true' starts publishing.
name: project.mqtt.publisher.setConfig

password.description: Vault password (requires username in same call)

description: Read the MQTT subscriber driver configuration (the UI driver -- this matches what would be used the next time io.connect runs with busType=mqtt). Password is never returned.
name: project.mqtt.subscriber.getConfig

description: Snapshot subscriber driver live state (isOpen, hostname, port).
name: project.mqtt.subscriber.getStatus

description: Patch one or more MQTT subscriber driver fields. Pass only the keys you want to change. Setting 'password' requires 'username' in the same call. Mutating fields while connected schedules a reconnect.
name: project.mqtt.subscriber.setConfig

autoKeepAlive.description: Let the client manage keepalive automatically

password.description: Subscriber password (requires username in same call)

description: Reset to a blank project (one default UART source, no groups, no datasets, no actions, no workspaces). DESTRUCTIVE: discards the loaded project. Use only when the user explicitly says "start over" or "new project". For starting a TYPED project, prefer project.template.apply -- it gives the user a useful skeleton instead of an empty canvas. Pass dryRun:true to see what the current project (wouldDiscard) looks like before wiping it.
name: project.new

dryRun.description: If true, return wouldDiscard + wouldCreate summary without committing.

description: Open a .ssproj or .json project file. Replaces the current project. Auto-switches operationMode to ProjectFile if it was QuickPlot or ConsoleOnly. Path must be absolute. Pass dryRun:true to read the file and return wouldDiscard + wouldApply summaries without loading.
name: project.open

dryRun.description: If true, parse the file and return its summary without loading.

filePath.description: Absolute path to project file (.json or .ssproj)

description: Add an output widget to a group (params: groupId, type)
name: project.outputWidget.add

groupId.description: Group id to add the widget to. Output widgets attach to a group's output panel.

description: Delete an output widget by id (params: groupId, widgetId)
name: project.outputWidget.delete

description: Compile an output-widget transmit function WITHOUT touching the live project. Verifies the script compiles and defines transmit(value); returns ok / compileError + line. The transmitFunction is **JavaScript only** and runs with the same injected Modbus/CAN helper globals + table API as the live widget. Pass inputValue (and hex:true for hex byte input) to also execute it once and return the produced bytes (outputHex + byteCount). Validate here BEFORE project.outputWidget.update.
name: project.outputWidget.dryRun

hex.description: Treat inputValue as space-separated hex bytes. Default false.

inputValue.description: Optional sample value to run transmit() against

description: Duplicate an output widget by id (params: groupId, widgetId)
name: project.outputWidget.duplicate

description: Read the current configuration of an output widget (params: groupId, widgetId). Returns title, icon, type, min/max/step/initialValue, transmitFunction. Use BEFORE rewriting the transmitFunction so you preserve the user's current ranges and labels.
name: project.outputWidget.get

description: Patch output-widget fields by id (params: groupId, widgetId, plus any of title, icon, transmitFunction, sourceId, txEncoding, monoIcon, minValue, maxValue, stepSize, initialValue). The transmitFunction is **JavaScript only** -- runs in QJSEngine to convert UI state into device bytes. **Call meta.fetchScriptingDocs{kind:'output_widget_js'} before authoring** for the function signature (transmit(value) returning a Uint8Array / string), the per-widget value semantics, and the injected Modbus/CAN helper globals (modbusWriteRegister / modbusWriteCoil / modbusWriteFloat / canSendFrame / canSendValue). Validate first with project.outputWidget.dryRun.
name: project.outputWidget.update

widgetId.description: Target widget index within the group

description: Compile a painter program WITHOUT touching the live project. Verifies that paint(ctx, w, h) exists and that the script compiles cleanly; does NOT actually render to a canvas. Returns ok / lastError. Params: code (string).
name: project.painter.dryRun

code.description: Painter source. Must define paint(ctx, w, h)

description: Get the painter widget JS for a group (params: groupId)
name: project.painter.getCode

description: Set the painter widget code for a group (params: groupId, code). **JavaScript only** -- painter scripts run in QJSEngine, not Lua. Available globals: ctx (2D canvas context, QPainter-like), w, h (canvas dimensions), datasetGetFinal(uid)/datasetGetRaw(uid). The entry point is paint(ctx, w, h) and an optional zero-arg onFrame() callback. Validate with project.painter.dryRun before setCode. **Always call meta.fetchScriptingDocs{kind:'painter_js'} first** for the full API surface and worked examples -- don't invent canvas methods from JS DOM Canvas, the surface is QPainter-shaped.
name: project.painter.setCode

code.description: Painter widget JS source. Must define paint(ctx, w, h) and may define a zero-arg onFrame(). Replaces any existing code for the group.

description: Write the current project to disk. Note: the AI runtime auto-saves after every successful mutating tool call within ~1 second, so you do NOT need to call this explicitly when editing. Call it ONLY when the user explicitly says "save" or wants a different file path -- in which case pass {filePath: "/abs/path"} for headless save-as.
name: project.save

askPath.description: Show native save dialog. Default false. AI runtime should almost always leave false -- the user already approved.

filePath.description: Absolute path to save to (headless save-as). Omit to save to the project's existing file path.

description: Rename the project (only the in-app title). Does not move or rename the .ssproj file on disk; auto-save still writes to the existing file path. To save to a different file, use project.save{filePath}.
name: project.setTitle

description:
Composite read of the active project: title, sources, groups + datasets, workspaces summary, and data tables summary -- in one round trip. Pass verbose=true for source-level frame settings and frame parser source. Prefer this over chaining list/get calls.
Every level (top, source, group, dataset) carries an `_explanations` object with prose translations of enum/bitflag fields (operationMode, busType, frameDetection, decoderMethod, frameParserLanguage, enabledOptions, transformLanguage) and a one-line summary of the project's operating shape -- read those first; the raw int fields stay alongside for machine processing.
Response includes `projectEpoch`, a monotonic counter that bumps on every structural mutation (group/dataset add, delete, move, source add/delete). Cache this value alongside any uniqueIds you pull from the snapshot, then pass `expectedProjectEpoch` to any later mutating command (dataset.update/move/delete, group.move/delete) -- the response will carry a stale_project warning if the project changed under you, so you can refetch before acting on now-shifted uniqueIds.
name: project.snapshot

verbose.description: Include frame parser source and source-level frame settings.

description: Add a new source (Commercial)
name: project.source.add

description: Delete a source (Commercial; sourceId >= 1)
name: project.source.delete

description: Get full source configuration (params: sourceId)
name: project.source.getConfig

description: Get per-source JS frame parser (params: sourceId)
name: project.source.getFrameParserCode

description: List all project sources
name: project.source.list

description: Set per-source JS frame parser (params: sourceId, code)
name: project.source.setFrameParserCode

description: Set multiple driver properties at once (params: sourceId, settings)
name: project.source.setProperties

description: Set a driver connection property (params: sourceId, key, value)
name: project.source.setProperty

description: Patch source fields by id (Commercial). Required: sourceId. Optional fields: title, busType, frameStart, frameEnd, checksumAlgorithm, frameDetection, decoderMethod, hexadecimalDelimiters.
name: project.source.update

checksumAlgorithm.description: Checksum algorithm name (none, crc8, crc16, crc32, ...)

frameEnd.description: Hex end delimiter for the frame

frameStart.description: Hex start delimiter for the frame

hexadecimalDelimiters.description: Interpret frameStart/frameEnd as hex byte sequences

description:
Replace the current project with a starter template -- the right first move when the user says "set me up an X project" rather than "add an X to my existing project". DESTRUCTIVE: discards any unsaved current state; auto-save will write the new template to disk shortly after. After applying, narrate what landed (groups, datasets) so the user knows what they got, then proceed with any follow-up edits the user requested. Available templates and their best fit:
  blank: empty project, one default UART source. Use when the user wants to build everything themselves.
  imu_uart: 9-DOF IMU (accelerometer X/Y/Z + gyro X/Y/Z + mag X/Y/Z) over UART, comma-separated, $ start / ; end. Replace the frame parser if your device frames differ.
  gps_uart_nmea: GPS over UART using NMEA 0183 ($GPGGA). Lat/lon/altitude/satellites/HDOP/fixQuality. Map widget configured.
  scope_multichannel_uart: 8 generic channels over UART, comma-separated, all plot-enabled. Adapt for ADC streams or sensor arrays.
  telemetry_udp: CSV body parser with 5 generic value channels, intended for UDP telemetry. The template does NOT configure the connection -- set up the UDP listener via io.network.* after applying.
  mqtt_subscriber (Pro): MQTT subscriber-mode skeleton. After applying, configure the broker via project.mqtt.subscriber.setConfig.
Pass dryRun:true to return wouldDiscard + wouldApply summaries without applying. Useful when the user is choosing between two templates.
name: project.template.apply

dryRun.description: If true, summarize the template without applying.

templateId.description: Template id from project.template.list (blank, imu_uart, gps_uart_nmea, scope_multichannel_uart, telemetry_udp, mqtt_subscriber)

description: List built-in starter project templates (id, title, description). Use this BEFORE project.template.apply when the user asks for a starter ("set me up an IMU project", "give me a GPS template").
name: project.template.list

description: Walk the loaded project and report inconsistencies (missing source references, parser compile errors, empty groups, duplicate dataset indexes, etc.). Returns {ok, issues:[{level, location, message}], issueCount, groupCount, sourceCount, actionCount}. Call before project.save when building a project programmatically.
name: project.validate

description: Create a new workspace (params: title="Workspace"). Returns new id.
name: project.workspace.add

description:
Pin a visualization tile onto a workspace. The tile renders the dashboard widget of the given widgetType fed by the given groupId. MANDATORY pre-flight: call project.group.list (for groupId + compatibleWidgetTypes) and project.workspace.list (for workspaceId) BEFORE calling this command. Calling it without that information produces validation errors. If your widgetType isn't in the group's compatibleWidgetTypes, first flip the matching dataset option via project.dataset.setOption / project.dataset.setOptions / project.dataset.update {graph|fft|led|waterfall}, then re-list and call addWidget.
Batching: for 5+ widget mutations, wrap calls in project.batch{ops:[{command:'project.workspace.addWidget', params:{...}}, ...]} -- workspace ops execute correctly inside batch and the whole burst lands as a single autosave (see meta.describeCommand{name:'project.batch'}). Returns widgetId for use with removeWidget.
name: project.workspace.addWidget

datasetId.description: OPTIONAL. For per-dataset widgets (plot/fft/bar/gauge/meter/compass/waterfall): pin the tile to THIS dataset within groupId. When omitted, the first dataset in the group with the matching option enabled is used. Ignored for group-level widgets (datagrid/multiplot/led/accelerometer/gyroscope/gps/plot3d/painter/imageview/output-panel).

groupId.description: Group.uniqueId from project.group.list (stable across reorders). NOT the positional array index and NOT Group.groupId.

relativeIndex.description: OPTIONAL and DISCOURAGED. Dashboard-level global index across all widgets of this type in the project (NOT a per-workspace or per-group counter). Omit it and the API computes the correct index from groupId (and datasetId for per-dataset widgets). Setting this manually is fragile and only useful when restoring a hand-crafted layout.

widgetType.description: Widget kind. PREFERRED form: a string slug -- 'plot', 'fft', 'bar', 'gauge', 'compass', 'led', 'datagrid', 'multiplot', 'accelerometer', 'gyroscope', 'gps', 'plot3d', 'imageview' (Pro), 'output-panel' (Pro), 'notification-log' (Pro), 'waterfall' (Pro), 'painter' (Pro). Integer DashboardWidget enum still accepted for back-compat (1=DataGrid, 2=MultiPlot, ... 9=Plot, 10=Bar, 11=Gauge, 12=Compass) but the strings are unambiguous and forwards-compatible.

workspaceId.description: Workspace id from project.workspace.list. IDs 1000-4999 are auto-generated (Overview=1000, AllData=1001, per-group >= 1002); user-created workspaces start at 5000.

description: Materialise synthetic workspaces into the customised set. No-op if already customised.
name: project.workspace.autoGenerate

description: Drop workspace widget refs that no longer resolve to a live group/dataset/widgetType. Scope: orphans only -- does NOT touch refs where the group still exists but compatibleWidgetTypes has changed (that's reversible state). Set dryRun:true to enumerate what would be removed without mutating.
name: project.workspace.cleanup

dryRun.description: If true, report what would be removed without mutating (default false)

description: Wipe every workspace in one call. Forces customize mode on and leaves an empty list. Use this instead of looping project.workspace.delete -- one approval card, one autosave flush. Follow with project.workspace.autoGenerate to restore the default Overview/AllData/per-group layout, or project.workspace.add + addWidget to build a custom layout. Pass dryRun:true to enumerate every workspace that would be removed before committing.
name: project.workspace.clearAll

dryRun.description: If true, return the list of workspaces that would be cleared. Auto-runs without an approval card.

description: Delete a workspace by id. Pass dryRun:true to preview the workspace + widgetCount that would be removed without committing.
name: project.workspace.delete

dryRun.description: If true, return what would be deleted without committing. Auto-runs without an approval card.

description: Return widget refs for a workspace (params: id)
name: project.workspace.get

description: Return the customizeWorkspaces flag
name: project.workspace.getCustomizeMode

description: List all workspaces with widget counts
name: project.workspace.list

description:
Remove a widget ref. Pass either {widgetId} from a prior addWidget/get response, or the legacy tuple {workspaceId, widgetType, groupId, relativeIndex}.
Batching: for 5+ widget removals, wrap calls in project.batch{ops:[...]} (see meta.describeCommand{name:'project.batch'}).
name: project.workspace.removeWidget

groupId.description: Group.uniqueId (stable across reorders) of the source group

widgetId.description: Opaque widget identifier in the form 'ws<workspaceId>:<slug>:g<groupId>:<relativeIndex>' (returned by project.workspace.addWidget and project.workspace.get). Provide EITHER widgetId OR the four tuple fields below.

description: Rename a workspace (params: id, title)
name: project.workspace.rename

description: Reorder user-defined workspaces (workspaceId >= 5000) in the taskbar. Pass every user workspace id in the desired order -- partial reorders are rejected to avoid silent corruption. System workspaces (auto-generated, ids 1000-4999) keep their original slots.
name: project.workspace.reorder

workspaceIds.description: Every user-workspace id (>= 5000), in the desired order. Must exactly match the set of current user workspaces.

description: Flip the customizeWorkspaces flag (params: enabled)
name: project.workspace.setCustomizeMode

description: Patch workspace fields (params: id; optional title, icon, description)
name: project.workspace.update

description.description: Free-text intent / audience note for the workspace (optional)

description: Walk workspace widget refs and report orphaned references (the group/dataset/widgetType combo no longer exists). Read-only -- does not modify project state or toggle customizeWorkspaces. Pair with project.workspace.cleanup to repair. Pass an optional workspaceId to limit scope; omit to validate all workspaces.
name: project.workspace.validate

description: Read the body of one bundled reference script (params: kind, id). The id is the `file` field from scripts.list (no extension).
name: scripts.get

description: List bundled reference scripts the dashboard ships with. Use this BEFORE writing painter / frame-parser / transform / output-widget code so you can adapt real templates instead of inventing patterns. Optional params: kind (filter to one kind: painter, frame_parser_js, frame_parser_lua, transform_js, transform_lua, output_widget_js).
name: scripts.list

kind.description: Optional kind filter -- one of: painter, frame_parser_js, frame_parser_lua, transform_js, transform_lua, output_widget_js. When omitted, all kinds are listed.

description: Create a new tag (params: label)
name: sessions.addTag

description: Assign a tag to a session (params: sessionId, tagId)
name: sessions.assignTag

description: Finalize and close the current session (no-op if none open)
name: sessions.close

description: Delete a stored session by id (params: sessionId)
name: sessions.delete

description: Delete a tag by id (params: tagId)
name: sessions.deleteTag

description: Export a stored session to CSV (async; params: sessionId)
name: sessions.exportToCsv

description: Return metadata + tags for one stored session (params: sessionId)
name: sessions.get

description: Return the canonical .db path for a given project title
name: sessions.getDbPath

projectTitle.description: Project title used for the session .db path

description: Return the session-recording flags (exportEnabled, isOpen)
name: sessions.getStatus

description: List all sessions stored in the currently open database
name: sessions.list

description: List all tags defined in the open database
name: sessions.listTags

description: Open a session database file for browsing (params: filePath)
name: sessions.openDatabase

filePath.description: Absolute path to the .db file to open

description: Remove a tag from a session (params: sessionId, tagId)
name: sessions.removeTag

description: Rename a tag (params: tagId, newLabel)
name: sessions.renameTag

description: Start the player on a stored session (params: sessionId)
name: sessions.replay

description: Turn session recording on/off (params: enabled)
name: sessions.setExportEnabled

enabled.description: Turn session recording on (true) or off (false)

description: Set free-form notes on a stored session (params: sessionId, notes)
name: sessions.setNotes

description: Launch a helper process (e.g. "python" with a script path). Control script only -- rejected over the network/SDK. workingDir defaults to the project directory. Returns a processId. The process is terminated automatically when the device disconnects, the project changes, or the app quits.
name: system.exec

program.description: Executable to run (resolved from PATH or an absolute path)

workingDir.description: Working directory (defaults to the project directory)

description: Terminate a managed helper process by id. Control script only.
name: system.kill

description: Return the loaded project's directory, file path, and file name. Use this to resolve paths relative to the .ssproj (e.g. a sibling Python script) before calling system.exec.
name: system.projectDir

description: List the helper processes currently managed by system.exec.
name: system.runningProcesses

description: Get serialized layout JSON from WindowManager
name: ui.window.getLayout

description: Get dashboard window status: activeGroupId, groupCount, autoLayoutEnabled
name: ui.window.getStatus

description: Get saved widget settings (params: widgetId string)
name: ui.window.getWidgetSettings

description: Get list of groups as [{id, text, icon}]
name: ui.window.listGroups

description: Get window states array [{id, state}] for current group
name: ui.window.listWindowStates

description: Load window layout from project
name: ui.window.loadLayout

description: Save current window layout to project
name: ui.window.saveLayout

description: Set active group by ID (params: groupId int)
name: ui.window.setActiveGroup

description: Set active group by index (params: index int)
name: ui.window.setActiveGroupIndex

description: Enable or disable auto layout (params: enabled bool)
name: ui.window.setAutoLayout

description: Apply a layout to WindowManager (params: layout object)
name: ui.window.setLayout

description: Set a widget setting (params: widgetId string, key string, value any)
name: ui.window.setWidgetSetting

description: Set window state (params: id int, state int: 0=normal,1=minimized,2=closed)
name: ui.window.setWindowState
